stem: Two sales representatives, Priya and Marcus, each earn a weekly base salary plus a fixed commission per unit sold. Priya earns a base salary of $300 per week and $11 per unit sold. Marcus earns a base salary of $60 per week and $15 per unit sold. For what number of units sold $s$ does Marcus earn exactly $120 more than Priya in a given week?

distractorRationale.A: Omits Priya's base salary from her earnings function: solves $(15s + 60) - 11s = 120$, giving $4s + 60 = 120$, so $4s = 60$ and $s = 15$.
distractorRationale.B: Ignores both representatives' base salaries entirely: solves $15s - 11s = 120$, giving $4s = 120$, so $s = 30$.
distractorRationale.C: Sets Marcus's earnings equal to Priya's (finds the break-even point instead of where Marcus earns $120 more): solves $15s + 60 = 11s + 300$, giving $4s = 240$, so $s = 60$.
